lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਤਾ ਨਾਲਿ ਵੈਰੁ ਕਮਾਵਦੇ ਦੁਸਟਾ ਨਾਲਿ ਮੋਹੁ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: santaa naal vair kamaavade dusattaa naal mohu piaar |
lines.english: They inflict their hatred upon the Saints, and they love the wicked sinners.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗੈ ਪਿਛੈ ਸੁਖੁ ਨਹੀ ਮਰਿ ਜੰਮਹਿ ਵਾਰੋ ਵਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: agai pichhai sukh nahee mar jameh vaaro vaar |
lines.english: They find no peace in either this world or the next; they are born only to die, again and again.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਹ ਕਾਲੇ ਤਿਨਾ ਨਿੰਦਕਾ ਤਿਤੁ ਸਚੈ ਦਰਬਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: muh kaale tinaa nindakaa tith sachai darabaar |
lines.english: The faces of these slanderers are blackened in the Court of the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਬੁਝਈ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਹੋਇ ਖੁਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: trisanaa kade na bujhee dubidhaa hoe khuaar |
lines.english: Their hunger is never satisfied, and they are ruined by duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮ ਵਿਹੂਣਿਆ ਨਾ ਉਰਵਾਰਿ ਨ ਪਾਰਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak naam vihooniaa naa uravaar na paar |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, without the Naam, they find no shelter on either this shore, or the one beyond. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਇਦੇ ਸੇ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਰਤੇ ਮਨ ਮਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: jo har naam dhiaaeide se har har naam rate man maahee |
lines.english: Those who meditate on the Lord's Name, are imbued with the Name of the Lord, Har, Har, in their minds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਾ ਮਨਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਇਕੁ ਅਰਾਧਿਆ ਤਿਨਾ ਇਕਸ ਬਿਨੁ ਦੂਜਾ ਕੋ ਨਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: jinaa man chit ik araadhiaa tinaa ikas bin doojaa ko naahee |
lines.english: For those who worship the One Lord in their conscious minds, there is no other than the One Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਗੁਣ ਨਿਤ ਗਾਵਦੇ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਇ ਗੁਣੀ ਸਮਝਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: har ke gun nit gaavade har gun gaae gunee samajhaahee |
lines.english: They continually sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord, and singing the Glories of the Glorious Lord, they are uplifted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇਈ ਪੁਰਖ ਹਰਿ ਸੇਵਦੇ ਜਿਨ ਧੁਰਿ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਲੇਖੁ ਲਿਖਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: seee purakh har sevade jin dhur masatak lekh likhaahee |
lines.english: They alone serve the Lord, upon whose foreheads such pre-ordained destiny is written.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡਿਆਈ ਵਡੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਾ ਗੁਰ ਪੂਰੈ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਸਮਾਹੀ ॥੧੭॥
lines.translit: vaddiaaee vaddee guramukhaa gur poorai har naam samaahee |17|
lines.english: Great is the greatness of the Gurmukhs, who, through the Perfect Guru, remain absorbed in the Lord's Name. ||17||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਗਾਖੜੀ ਸਿਰੁ ਦੀਜੈ ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: satigur kee sevaa gaakharree sir deejai aap gavaae |
lines.english: It is very difficult to serve the True Guru; offer your head, and eradicate self-conceit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਦਿ ਮਰਹਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਨਾ ਮਰਹਿ ਤਾ ਸੇਵਾ ਪਵੈ ਸਭ ਥਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: sabad mareh fir naa mareh taa sevaa pavai sabh thaae |
lines.english: One who dies in the Word of the Shabad shall never have to die again; his service is totally approved.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਰਸ ਪਰਸਿਐ ਪਾਰਸੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਸਚਿ ਰਹੈ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: paaras parasiai paaras hovai sach rahai liv laae |
lines.english: Touching the philosopher's stone, one becomes the philosopher's stone, which transforms lead into gold; remain lovingly attached to the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਪੂਰਬਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਤਿਸੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: jis poorab hovai likhiaa tis satigur milai prabh aae |
lines.english: One who has such pre-ordained destiny, comes to meet the True Guru and God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਲੁ ਕੁਮਹਲੁ ਨ ਜਾਣਨੀ ਮੂਰਖ ਅਪਣੈ ਸੁਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: mehal kumahal na jaananee moorakh apanai suaae |
lines.english: The fools do not know the difference between good and bad; they are deceived by their self-interests.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗਣਤੈ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਨਾ ਮਿਲੈ ਜਿਸੁ ਬਖਸੇ ਸੋ ਪਵੈ ਥਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak ganatai sevak naa milai jis bakhase so pavai thaae |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Lord's servant does not meet Him because of his own account; he alone is acceptable, whom the Lord forgives. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਦਾ ਸਚੇ ਕਾ ਭਉ ਮਨਿ ਵਸੈ ਤਾ ਸਭਾ ਸੋਝੀ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: sadaa sache kaa bhau man vasai taa sabhaa sojhee paae |
lines.english: The Fear of God is always on their minds, and so they come to understand everything.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਦੁ ਚੀਨਹਿ ਤਾ ਮਹਲੁ ਲਹਹਿ ਜੋਤੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਸਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: sabad cheeneh taa mehal laheh jotee jot samaae |
lines.english: But if they contemplate the Word of the Shabad, they obtain the Mansion of the Lord's Presence, and their light merges in the Light.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਅਪਣੈ ਘਰਿ ਵਰਤਦਾ ਆਪੇ ਲਏ ਮਿਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: satigur apanai ghar varatadaa aape le milaae |
lines.english: The True Guru is pervading the homes within; He Himself blends them with the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਸਭ ਪੂਰੀ ਪਈ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰੇ ਰਜਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak satigur miliai sabh pooree pee jis no kirapaa kare rajaae |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, they meet the True Guru, and all their desires are fulfilled, if the Lord grants His Grace and so wills. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੰਨੁ ਧਨੁ ਭਾਗ ਤਿਨਾ ਭਗਤ ਜਨਾ ਜੋ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਾ ਹਰਿ ਮੁਖਿ ਕਹਤਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: dhan dhan bhaag tinaa bhagat janaa jo har naamaa har mukh kahatiaa |
lines.english: Blessed, blessed is the good fortune of those devotees, who, with their mouths, utter the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਧਨੁ ਭਾਗ ਤਿਨਾ ਸੰਤ ਜਨਾ ਜੋ ਹਰਿ ਜਸੁ ਸ੍ਰਵਣੀ ਸੁਣਤਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: dhan dhan bhaag tinaa sant janaa jo har jas sravanee sunatiaa |
lines.english: Blessed, blessed is the good fortune of those Saints, who, with their ears, listen to the Lord's Praises.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਧਨੁ ਭਾਗ ਤਿਨਾ ਸਾਧ ਜਨਾ ਹਰਿ ਕੀਰਤਨੁ ਗਾਇ ਗੁਣੀ ਜਨ ਬਣਤਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: dhan dhan bhaag tinaa saadh janaa har keeratan gaae gunee jan banatiaa |
lines.english: Blessed, blessed is the good fortune of those holy people, who sing the Kirtan of the Lord's Praises, and so become virtuous.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਧਨੁ ਭਾਗ ਤਿਨਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਾ ਜੋ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਲੈ ਮਨੁ ਜਿਣਤਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: dhan dhan bhaag tinaa guramukhaa jo gurasikh lai man jinatiaa |
lines.english: Blessed, blessed is the good fortune of those Gurmukhs, who live as Gursikhs, and conquer their minds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਬਿੰਦੈ ਤਿਸ ਦਾ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਤੁ ਰਹੈ ਏਕ ਸਬਦਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: braham bindai tis daa brahamat rahai ek sabad liv laae |
lines.english: One who knows God, and who lovingly focuses his attention on the One Word of the Shabad, keeps his spirituality intact.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭ ਦੂ ਵਡੇ ਭਾਗ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਾ ਕੇ ਜੋ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਣੀ ਸਿਖ ਪੜਤਿਆ ॥੧੮॥
lines.translit: sabh doo vadde bhaag gurasikhaa ke jo gur charanee sikh parratiaa |18|
lines.english: But the greatest good fortune of all, is that of the Guru's Sikhs, who fall at the Guru's feet. ||18||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਵ ਨਿਧੀ ਅਠਾਰਹ ਸਿਧੀ ਪਿਛੈ ਲਗੀਆ ਫਿਰਹਿ ਜੋ ਹਰਿ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਸਦਾ ਵਸਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: nav nidhee atthaarah sidhee pichhai lageea fireh jo har hiradai sadaa vasaae |
lines.english: The nine treasures and the eighteen spiritual powers of the Siddhas follow him, who keeps the Lord enshrined in his heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਨਾਉ ਨ ਪਾਈਐ ਬੁਝਹੁ ਕਰਿ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: bin satigur naau na paaeeai bujhahu kar veechaar |
lines.english: Without the True Guru, the Name is not found; understand this, and reflect upon it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਪੂਰੈ ਭਾਗਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਏ ਜੁਗ ਚਾਰਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak poorai bhaag satigur milai sukh paae jug chaar |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, through perfect good destiny, one meets the True Guru, and finds peace, throughout the four ages. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਗਭਰੂ ਕਿਆ ਬਿਰਧਿ ਹੈ ਮਨਮੁਖ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਭੁਖ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa gabharoo kiaa biradh hai manamukh trisanaa bhukh na jaae |
lines.english: Whether he is young or old, the self-willed manmukh cannot escape hunger and thirst.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਬਦੇ ਰਤਿਆ ਸੀਤਲੁ ਹੋਏ ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh sabade ratiaa seetal hoe aap gavaae |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs are imbued with the Word of the Shabad; they are at peace, having lost their self-conceit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਦਰੁ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਿ ਸੰਤੋਖਿਆ ਫਿਰਿ ਭੁਖ ਨ ਲਗੈ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: andar tripat santokhiaa fir bhukh na lagai aae |
lines.english: They are satisfied and satiated within; they never feel hungry again.
